items.headline: United States and Iran agree to two-week ceasefire after Pakistani mediation
items.body: Washington and Tehran suspended military operations hours before a deadline that threatened sweeping infrastructure strikes. Pakistan brokered talks set for Islamabad on April 10 to finalize terms that include reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Cameroon president names son vice president and head of armed forces
items.body: Paul Biya appointed Franck Emmanuel Biya to three top positions consolidating executive and military power within the family. The decree follows disputed elections and intensifies scrutiny over succession in a country Biya has ruled since 1982.

items.headline: Colombia government threatens new economic emergency as budget crisis deepens
items.body: President Petro announced plans to submit emergency decree and tax reform after Congress rejected earlier fiscal proposals. Constitutional court previously suspended a December emergency leaving a gap in revenue to fund the 2026 budget.

items.headline: Morocco hosts GITEX Africa as continent positions for digital economy expansion
items.body: Over 1,450 exhibitors from 145 countries gathered in Marrakech for Africa's largest technology summit focused on AI-driven transformation. Organizers frame connectivity and digital infrastructure as critical to bridging development gaps across the continent.
